Viva.com envisions to provide businesses a value\-driven platform to accelerate the adoption of the latest technology. **Role Overview:** On\-site Representatives are responsible for promoting company's services remotely while providing merchants with the necessary support to ensure business continuity and optimal service usage. **Key Responsibilities:** * Promote company's services through remote interactions; * Assist merchants with verification and activation processes; * Monitor and analyze portfolio performance; * Provide remote support and consultation for existing customers; * Perform scheduled and self\-initiated activities; * Ensure new merchants in the assigned portfolio complete registration; * Enter and maintain up to date client information into the company's database and keep records with relevant data; * Analyze market trends and customer feedback to refine strategies; * Support existing merchants in maintaining active usage; * Escalate issues by opening cases for problems beyond their resolution scope; * Present product features and benefits to clients in a compelling manner. **Requirements** * 2\+ years of experience in business development within a fast\-paced environment preferably a financial services institution or similar SaaS solution; * Good knowledge of relevant computer programs (e.g. CRM software), social networking (LinkedIn) and telephone systems; * Excellent communication skills (verbal and written); * Excellent level of English; * Ability to work independently and under pressure; * A positive approach to objection handling; * Good organizational skills and attention to detail **Benefits** * Bonus, capped at an additional 18 months salary per annum * Private medical health insurance * 25 Days holiday \+ bank holidays * A diverse multi\-cultural working environment * Local office based in Dublin * 9am\-5:30pm working hours Monday to Friday

title: Clinical Services Psychologist  /Psychotherapist
description: **Aware Clinical Services Psychologist/Psychotherapist** **Job Overview:** We are seeking a compassionate, innovative, and enthusiastic psychologist or psychotherapist to join our friendly and dedicated team in the NGO sector. This is a new role offering an exciting opportunity to support the strategic clinical leadership across the range of services offered and shape high\-quality mental health service delivery for people living with depression, bipolar, and anxiety. **About Aware:** Aware is the national organisation providing free support, education, information and therapeutic services to people impacted by depression, bipolar and anxiety. We play a vital role in offering free mental health services across the country to the public. Aware works with a large team of mental health professionals and volunteers and training partners to deliver our wide range of vital services. **Our Vision** Aware undertakes to create a society where people affected by stress, depression, bipolar disorder and mood related conditions are understood, supported, free from stigma, and are encouraged to access appropriate therapies. **Our Values** Excellence, Compassion, Accountability and Integrity. **About the role** Reporting to Aware’s Clinical Director (Senior Clinical Psychologist), key aspects of the role include (but are not limited to): * To ensure the clinical effectiveness of Aware counselling and psychoeducation programmes by supporting clinical audit and other service development activities related to the services. * To contribute to creating a psychologically informed framework of service delivery across Aware Services * To provide clinical consultation/guidance and, where required, clinical supervision to Aware clinicians and service managers * To undertake direct assessment and triage with new service users to the Aware Counselling Service, in line with best practice. * To hold a small caseload, offering brief psychological therapy to adults presenting with mild to moderate depression and anxiety * To conduct risk assessments and implement risk management plans as appropriate * To contribute to research in line with the Aware Research Strategy and overall objectives * To plan and deliver teaching and training on workplace mental health workshops in line with best practice and current evidence base. * To represent Aware in public, policy, and sector\-wide mental health discussions. * To work with the communications department utilising the sector wide research / insights to inform our evidence\-informed communications strategy * Support the Clinical Director in offering external\-facing psychological leadership, specifically engaging state bodies and medical stakeholders to represent, promote, and advance Aware strategic objectives. Essential Skills and Experience: * Have an Irish post\-graduate professional psychology qualification (Doctorate or equivalent) accredited by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. Candidates qualified overseas must provide evidence of validation by the Department of Health at the time of application. Or * Psychotherapist fully accredited by the Irish Council for Psychotherapy (ICP) or the Irish Association for Counselling and Psychotherapy (IACP) * For psychologists, at least 5 years’ experience in professional psychology, with no less than 3 years spent in gaining the post graduate qualification and at least 1 year full time in post qualification experience as a professional psychologist. * For psychotherapists a minimum of five years post qualification/accreditation experience working in health and social care, therapeutic or related organisational settings supporting people with depression and anxiety * Commitment to evidence\-based practice and client\-centred care. * Effective presentation and facilitation skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner to a wide range of audiences * Excellent communication, teamwork, and interpersonal skills. * Comfortable with a wide range of IT systems. Desirable: * Experience of the not\-for\-profit sector * Experience of delivering and evaluating brief therapeutic interventions * Interest in service development and creation of psychoeducational and therapeutic resources in mental health Package * This is a permanent post. * Salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The salary range for this role will be €52,000 – €60,000 pro rata (0\.6 FTE / 22\.5 hours per week) * Aware is an equal opportunities employer. * The role is office based in 9 Upper Leeson Street, Dublin 4, offering a hybrid work model To apply please submit a one\-page cover letter outlining your suitability for the role and a CV (2 pages max) to info@aware.ie *before 5pm on 12th May* . Informal enquiries to Dr Susan Brannick on 01 661 7211\. Interviews will be held the week commencing 18th May.

title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist - Pectus Service
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist \- Pectus Service** **Department: Physiotherapy** **Reports to: Allied Therapy Manager** **Date: 2026** 10 hours per week **Overall Purpose of Job** We will provide exceptional patient care in an environment where quality, respect, caring and compassion are at the centre of all we do. **Key Responsibilities** **1\. PROFESSIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:** **1\.1\.1\.** Co\-ordinate and deliver an effective and efficient Pectus Physiotherapy Service, based on assessment of needs. **1\.1\.2\.** Develop and administer the highest quality of service by identifying and implementing operational procedures in line with best practice standards. **1\.1\.3\.** Keep abreast with new developments, skills and technology to ensure continuing competence in the job. **1\.1\.4\.** Enhance his/her skills in order to initiate and respond to changes in the work environment as additional roles may be demanded e.g. data analysis, clinical audit, continued professional development and research. **2\. MANAGEMENT RESPONSIBILITIES:** **2\.1\.1\.** To participate in the management of the Physiotherapy Service as a member of the Senior Physiotherapy Management team in co\-operation with the Allied Therapy Manager. **2\.1\.2\.** To create and promote good working relationships through open communication. **2\.1\.3\.** To contribute to and participate in the staff induction, staff appraisal and in\-service training programmes. **2\.1\.4\.** To actively participate in and promote continuing education and research activities consistent with the position. **2\.1\.5\.** To maintain appropriate records and statistics sufficient for organisational and legal purposes to include work related injuries/incidents. **2\.1\.6\.** Toregularly review and update Pectus Service Policies and Standard Operating Procedure documents. **2\.1\.7\.** To assist the Allied Therapy Manager in achieving Quality and Accreditation requirements for Physiotherapy. **2\.1\.8\.** To provide in intra disciplinary education to promote physiotherapy service in Beacon Hospital. **2\.1\.9\.** To provide flexible assistance to the Allied Therapy Manager in delivering clinical care and in operational roles such as Policy and Procedure Document updates, Beacon Hospital committee participation where required. **3\. STAFF MANAGEMENT** **3\.1\.1\.** To lead by example a professional, dedicated and punctual team. **3\.1\.2\.** To encourage professional development of team members through promotion of self\-learning, formal courses and in\-service training. **NOTE 1\.** The hours of work are at the discretion of the Physiotherapy Manager and in line with service needs. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Candidates must hold a degree in Physiotherapy, be registered with CORU and membership of the Irish Society of Chartered Physiotherapists is desirable. **Experience** (Amount and Type) Candidates must hold at least 5 years post graduate experience in the area of Orthopaedic or Musculoskeletal physiotherapy. A suitable postgraduate qualification in Orthopaedics, Musculoskeletal practice is desirable. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** In addition to the responsibilities and core competencies of a senior physiotherapist: ***The candidate must have/display the following attributes:*** Applies expert clinical knowledge and reasoning skills in assessment, planning, Implementation and evaluation of patient interventions Carries out clinical skills in the area in keeping with current evidence\-based practice Delivers a holistic patient focused approach and embraces the MDT perspective Applies evidence\-based outcome measures to evaluate efficacy of treatment and Quality of care Balances clinical demands with other responsibilities. * Demonstrates an educational role for example as a 'mentor. * Participates in research. * Generates new knowledge and skills in the specified area of Physiotherapy. * Disseminates advanced ethical awareness and responsibilities in specific areas. * Demonstrates leadership and an understanding of service planning. Demonstrates a critical understanding of the context in which practice occurs for example social, political and inter professional collaboration. Strong evidence of Continuing Professional Development \- Strength and Conditioning etc. is desirable **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager of hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: Cloud DevOps Engineer
description: What you are expected to have: * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field with 10\-15 years of overall IT experience. * Minimum of 5\-10 years of relevant experience in cloud engineering and DevOps. * In\-depth knowledge and hands\-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. * Proficient in infrastructure\-as\-code (IaC) tools, such as Terraform or CloudFormation or ARM. * Solid understanding of containerization technologies, particularly Docker and Kubernetes especially EKS, ECS and AKS. * Experience in implementing and managing CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI, Bamboo or CircleCI. * Strong scripting skills in languages like Python, Shell, or PowerShell. * Familiarity with configuration management tools such as Ansible or Puppet. * Knowledge of networking principles and security best practices in cloud environments. * Deploying AI solutions on AWS using AWS Bedrock, Sagemaker etc. * Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (Preferred). * Relevant certifications in cloud platforms and DevOps tools (Preferred). * Experience in an IT consulting services environment (Preferred). * Advanced proficiency in cloud\-native services and serverless architectures (Preferred). * Knowledge of monitoring and logging tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, or Splunk (Preferred). * Expertise in optimizing cloud costs and resource management (Preferred). * Strong communication skills with the ability to convey technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders (Preferred). * Active participation in the open\-source community or contributions to relevant projects (Preferred). You are will do: * Collaborate with clients and internal teams to understand and analyze their technical requirements. * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure solutions with a focus on scalability, security, and performance. * Implement and optimize CI/CD pipelines using DevOps best practices. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to cloud infrastructure and deployments. * Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies in cloud computing and DevOps. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure successful delivery of cloud\-based projects. * Provide technical support and guidance to junior team members.

title: Cloud Security Engineer (Kubernetes / Multi-Cloud)
description: We’re looking for a hands\-on Cloud Security Analyst with deep expertise in cloud\-native and container security. This role sits at the core of securing large\-scale, multi\-cloud and Kubernetes\-driven environments. You will play a critical role in defining security standards, implementing hardened baselines, and enhancing detection and response capabilities—working closely with DevOps, Networking, and SOC teams to embed security into every layer of design and automation. 🧩 Key Responsibilities Strengthen and optimize cloud\-native security controls across IAM, network security (WAF, VPC), and data protection (KMS, Vault) Deploy and manage CSPM, CNAPP, and CWPP solutions to maintain continuous security posture across multi\-cloud and Kubernetes environments Integrate cloud telemetry (GCP Audit Logs, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Kubernetes audit logs) into SIEM/SOAR platforms for centralized visibility Define and enforce cloud security architectures and guardrails aligned with Zero Trust and Least Privilege principles Develop and maintain Security\-as\-Code practices using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Bicep, including policy\-as\-code frameworks Own and enhance the security posture of containerized environments (GKE, AKS, EKS), including image, registry, and runtime security Harden Kubernetes clusters through RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and secure configurations Lead cloud security incident response activities including triage, containment, and forensic investigations Continuously monitor emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack vectors within cloud\-native ecosystems 🎯 Required Qualifications Experience 8\+ years in Information Security Minimum 8\+ years in Cloud Security At least 6\+ years working with Kubernetes / container security Cloud Expertise Hands\-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (GCP and Azure preferred; AWS/OCI a plus) Container \& Kubernetes Security Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation Security Frameworks \& Compliance Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks Certifications (Preferred) CISSP CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect AWS Certified Security – Specialty Soft Skills Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams Experience working in global or multi\-national environments ⭐ Nice to Have Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI * Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused credentials

title: Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship
description: **Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship – Boliden Tara Mines** **Build a skilled, future\-ready career with support, purpose and real opportunities to grow.** At Boliden Tara Mines, we provide the metals essential to improve society for generations to come. We are guided by our values of Care, Courage and Responsibility, and by our ambition to be the most climate\-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. If you enjoy problem solving, technology, and learning how complex systems work \- this apprenticeship could be for you. **Thinking about your next step ?** A **Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship** at Tara Mines offers a practical, structured way to **earn while you learn** , gain a nationally recognised qualification, and build skills that are in demand across many industries. On successful completion, apprentices will achieve a **QQI Advanced Certificate – Level 6** , a nationally recognised qualification valued across multiple industries. You don’t need previous experience \- we provide training, mentoring and support from day one. **What You’ll Learn And Do** **Construction Plant Fitters** support with the maintenance and reliability of mobile plant and equipment used across our mining operations. With guidance from experienced mentors, you’ll learn how these machines operate and why safety, quality and teamwork are critical in everything we do. During your apprenticeship, you’ll be supported to learn how to: * Diagnose faults and carry out repairs on mobile plant and equipment * Perform routine servicing and preventative maintenance to keep equipment operating safely and efficiently * Complete inspections and safety checks in line with mining regulations and site standards * Use modern maintenance systems and technology, including the CMMS system (Maximo) * Develop strong problem‑solving skills through proactive fault‑finding and repair activities **Your support and development** Boliden Tara Mines offers world‑class technical training, along with competitive pay and excellent rewards as you progress and develop your skills. You will be part of a safety\-focused, inclusive team, supported by experienced and qualified craftspeople who will: * Coach and guide you throughout your apprenticeship * Help you build confidence in your technical skills * Encourage questions and continuous learning We take pride in creating an environment where apprentices feel supported, respected and safe to learn. **Entry Requirements** * Aged 18 years or over * Leaving Certificate with a minimum of five passes at Ordinary Level, including English and Maths **Skills And Qualities We Look For** * Curiosity and interest in how things work * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and ask questions * A positive, proactive approach * An interest in technology, engineering or problem solving **Practical details** * 39 hours per week, Monday to Friday * Some flexibility required in line with operational needs * A structured apprenticeship combining on\-the\-job learning with off\-site training **Opportunities within the wider Boliden Group** An apprenticeship with Boliden Tara Mines offers more than a qualification — it’s the start of a long‑term career. Graduates can grow onsite and across the **wider Boliden Group** , with skills valued nationally and internationally. **Inclusion matters to us** At Boliden Tara Mines, we are committed to a workplace where women and men feel welcome, supported and confident to succeed. We encourage applications from all backgrounds, including people who may not have previously considered a career in Electrical Instrumentation If you’re interested but unsure whether you meet all the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. If this career path interests you, we welcome your application. **Closing date: 19 June 2026** **If this opportunity interests you, we welcome your application via Boliden Careers.** Important! If you are a current employee within Boliden you shouldn't complete the application at this site, please log on to your Workday account and apply through "Find Jobs".

title: Content Marketing Specialist
description: Position Overview We are looking for a creative and enthusiastic Content Marketing Specialist to join our team on a part\-time basis. In this role, you will help create engaging content across social media, or blogs to grow our brand and build online communities. This is a flexible part\-time position ideal for someone who wants to gain hands\-on experience in content marketing.Key Responsibilities * Plan and create high\-quality content (posts, articles, or videos, graphics) for social media platforms and our website * Manage social media posting and community engagement * Help develop content calendars and support marketing campaigns * Work closely with the marketing team to support overall brand goals * Stay up\-to\-date with the latest content trends and social media best practices Requirements * Social media experience OR a major in computer\-related field (e.g., Computer Science, Digital Media, Information Technology, etc.) * Excellent writing and editing skills in English * Must have your own personal social media account(s) with a certain number of followers (at least 500\+ real followers across platforms) * Ability to create visual content using tools like Canva, CapCut, or similar, with proficiency in AI\-powered editing and clipping software (e.g., AI features in CapCut, Adobe Firefly, Runway, or equivalent tools) * Good analytical skills and basic familiarity with social media insights * No full\-time work experience required – recent graduates or students are welcome What We Offer * Competitive hourly pay * Flexible working hours (part\-time, approximately 15 hours per week) * Opportunity to grow your skills and portfolio * Creative and supportive work environment If you love creating content, have a strong personal social media presence, and want to help brands tell their stories, we’d love to hear from you! Please send your resume and links to your personal social media accounts when applying. Pay: From €18\.00 per hour Work Location: Remote

title: Corporate Actions Analyst
description: **Job Description** The Corporate Actions Analyst is responsible for the daily processing associated with Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions. The successful candidate will assist with co\-ordinating the flow of required information between various stakeholders, including the Front Office, Portfolio Managers, Fund Accounting, Treasurer’s Offices and Custodian banks. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience The **Purpose** of Your Role The Global Corporate Actions team is responsible for all aspects of Corporate Actions processing. This includes Income, Mandatory and Voluntary events. The Corporate Actions Analyst is responsible for the daily tasks associated with processing Corporate Actions. This includes fulfilling the departments policies associated with corporate actions reporting and data management. The successful applicant will interact with many groups within the Fidelity organization and represent the business to customers and business partners. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality Soliciting of Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes * Respond to research inquiries received from internal stakeholders and external vendors How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Corporate Services Assistant
description: **Overview Of Walkers** Walkers is an international firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to the global financial services community. Walkers Professional Services ("WPS") sets a “best in class” standard in the provision of corporate services to aircraft leasing and structured finance special purpose vehicles in Ireland. WPS is led by a team of over 50 highly skilled professionals drawn from accounting, audit, company secretarial, compliance and legal fields. The team combines a depth of specialised experience with a focus on consistently delivering high quality results to clients at all times. At WPS, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone can feel comfortable, happy and confident to be themselves. We believe that a diverse and inclusive business is more collaborative and innovative and provides great opportunity for our staff which in turn, allows us to better serve the needs of our clients. Additionally, we actively encourage participation in internal committees such as Corporate \& Social Responsibility and Diversity and Inclusion along with other firm wide initiatives. Walkers is an equal opportunities employer. **The Role** WPS is currently seeking a Corporate Services Assistant to join our dynamic and growing transaction team in Dublin. In this role, you will be part of a busy, client facing, professionally qualified team. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. Please note, this is a fully onsite role and applicants must be willing and able to work five days per week in our Dublin office. **Duties And Responsibilities** You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Assisting senior transaction executives and directors in the management of a portfolio of SPVs involved in structured finance and aircraft leasing; * Building and maintaining strong client relationships, ensuring a high quality, responsive and professional service to clients at all times; * Leading the process of on\-boarding new clients; * Assisting with a variety of company secretarial duties including coordinating board meetings, company incorporations, annual compliance, filings with the Companies Registration Office and other ad hoc duties; * Establishing and maintaining statutory books and records; * Assisting in the incorporation and setting up of new entities; * Attending board meetings and drafting non\-transactional board minutes; * Liaising with investment managers, arrangers, lessors, lawyers and other relevant parties; * Assisting with bank account openings, FATCA registrations, EMIR obligations and other necessary SPV transaction management tasks as required; * Contributing to internal projects and initiatives arising from changes in statutory or regulatory obligations; and * Ensuring compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements and a positive office environment. **Knowledge And Experience** * Prior office experience would be an advantage; * A university law or business graduate, with an excellent academic background; * A desire to work in a client facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance; * Strong communication skills, an ability to think on their feet, excellent attention to detail and exceptional organisational skills are essential for this multi\-client role; * Conscientious, organised and a team player who can work on their own and as part of a wider team; and * Enthusiastic in nature, with a real interest in learning and developing their skills. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 23 days annual leave (increasing to 25 days with service) * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme

title: Corporate Solicitor
description: We are working with a market\-leading national law firm who are seeking an Associate Solicitor to join their Corporate team within their Dublin office. **The Role** The successful candidate will join an award\-winning, fast\-growing Corporate practice, working on a full spectrum of corporate services, including M\&A, joint ventures, and private equity deals. The candidate’s duties will deliver expert legal advice on a range of corporate transactions, with a cross\-border focus, acting for Irish, UK\-based and international equity sponsors and lenders. The candidate will also demonstrate a willingness to participate in business development initiatives while managing client relationships. **Essential Criteria** * 3\+ PQE; * Strong background in corporate services, M\&A, and private equity in a medium to large commercial firm; * Excellent commercial awareness; * Outstanding academics; * Technically excellent; * Negotiation skills; * Willingness to become involved in business development; * Ability to work independently and as a team. **Benefits** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Generous benefits package; * Extensive opportunities for career development; * Excellent client base; * Hybrid working; * Inclusive and friendly culture. Please contact Andrew Currie on 0892015064 or email andrew.currie@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: Customer Growth Associate, Early Careers, Google Customer Solutions (German Market)
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or related field. * Ability to communicate in English and German fluently to engage with clients in the region. **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to own an advertising or media sales portfolio in Germany, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. **About The Job** Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. As a Customer Growth Associate, you bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You'll act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. Building trusted relationships with customers along the way, you'll uncover their business needs and translate them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI\-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. **Responsibilities** * Deliver against ambitious quarterly business growth and product growth goals. * Establish relationships via phone calls or email marketing and conduct regular phone consultations with clients to advise them on how to get the best return on their Google Ads investment. * Own a portfolio of small businesses by thoroughly understanding growth drivers identifying opportunities for growth, managing risks and building quarterly plans for achievement. * Drive customer growth by delivering outstanding customer sales experience and achieving customer business and marketing objectives. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Cyber Security Risk Analyst to join a growing team on a permanent basis. This role requires 1 day per week onsite. The Cyber Security Risk Analyst will be responsible for establishing and maintaining a strong security posture across the organisation. The team defines security strategies, frameworks, and controls, and ensures their effective implementation to protect information assets and manage security risks. **Responsibilities** * Act as a primary point of contact for information security matters related to service delivery and operational activities. * Promote awareness and understanding of information security principles, policies, and processes across the organisation. * Identify, assess, and monitor information security risks in collaboration with risk owners, ensuring appropriate controls are applied throughout the service and system lifecycle. * Evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ensure alignment with organisational policies, regulatory obligations, and recognised industry standards. * Develop, maintain, and improve information security policies, standards, and procedures. * Support third\-party risk management activities, including defining and assessing security requirements for suppliers and outsourced services. * Lead or contribute to security initiatives and improvement projects, working with cross\-functional teams to enhance security processes and increase automation where appropriate. **Skills / Experience Required** * A degree in computer science, information technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * 5\+ years of experience in information security or related IT roles, with strong exposure to risk management, security frameworks, and control design. * Broad knowledge of information security technologies, architectures, and operational processes, including information security management systems and industry standards (e.g. ISO 27001, COBIT, DORA, NIS2\). * Experience working with data, reporting, automation, or AI\-enabled tools to support security processes. * Experience managing or assessing security risks associated with third\-party suppliers, cloud services, and outsourced environments. * Proven ability to manage projects and influence stakeholders in complex organisational environments. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, professional documentation and communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences. **Benefits** Hybrid Working \- Only 1 Day per week required on site Healthcare Bonus Childcare Allowance Pension Sub Canteen

title: Data Analyst
description: Are you a Data Analyst looking to make your next opportunity? I may have the job for you! **About Your New Employer** * Join a company that leverages one of the richest proprietary data sets in the world. * Work with a high\-calibre, passionate team dedicated to leading high\-impact projects. * Utilise advanced data tools and technologies to unlock customer value and drive business insights. **About Your New Job as a Program Manager** * Use unique data to power innovative tools addressing business challenges. * Write SQL/Presto/Trino/Spark queries to extract necessary data and use Tableau for data visualisation. * Take existing queries or narratives and optimise them for efficiency, building in automated checks and controls. * Handle support requests to ensure a bug\-free user experience and implement enhancements to internal data tools. **What Skills You Need as a Program Manager** * 3\+ years of experience with data analysis. * Previous experience working with SQL and data visualisation tools (e.g., Presto/Trino, Spark SQL, Tableau). * Comfortable working with PowerPoint and Excel. * Ability to turn data into insights and present to stakeholders. * Experience managing complex, multi\-workstream programs. * Expert knowledge of SQL, Hadoop (Presto/Spark), Git, and Tableau. **What’s on Offer as a Program Manager** * €58,000 \- €62,000 Annual Salary * 11\-month Temporary Contract * Hybrid Work \- One Day in the office per week **What’s Next** If you think this job is for you, apply now, or if you have any questions about the role or other IT Contract Jobs in Ireland, message me, Aaron Thornton, or click on apply. Looking forward to talking to some great candidates!

title: Data Analyst, Financial Enablement
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Internal System Org is at the core of Stripe to empower mission\-critical financial domains including Finance \& Strategy (F\&S), Accounting, Tax, Treasury, Product Experience, GTM. We are the Financial Enablement team, acting as the centralized data analytics team for Internal System Org. Enable and empower F\&S, GTM, Accounting, Product teams to achieve business goals, through dataset development, data analytics support, dashboards, AI, and operation efficiency enhancements. What you’ll do **Responsibilities** * Perform data analysis, identify trends, conduct research, build visualizations, all to deliver objective, critical insights that fulfill team stakeholders’ business needs * Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including Finance \& Strategy, Accounting, Product, Tax, User billing, Seller System, and Sales Ops teams, to align on financial goals and drive revenue growth * Be the data strategy partner for our business stakeholders * Reconcile complex dataset across complex finance systems * Coordinate cross\-functional projects to identify and drive operational efficiencies that support our stakeholders ability to run more efficiently and effectively * Work closely with Engineering and Product teams to help define product solutions * Leverage AI tools to improve team work efficiency * Conduct internal knowledge sharing sessions to strengthen team Who you are You’re passionate about solving business problems and making a direct impact on business. You thrive in environments with many moving parts and quickly pick up new domain knowledge and technologies (e.g. AI). We’re seeking someone with excellent communication and advanced SQL/analytics abilities. Strong background in Accounting/Finance or prior AI experience is highly preferred, though these can also be developed on the job. **Minimum Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in STEM major (i.e. Computer Science, Math, Stat, Engineering, etc.) and 4\+ years of prior working experience in data analysis fields * Strong analytical ability, proficiency with SQL (minimum of 3 years advanced SQL) and strong understanding of data relationships * Experience working with extremely large datasets * Outstanding communication skills * Expertise working cross\-functionally to drive business and operational goals * Excellent project management skills * Domain knowledge in Finance/Accounting/GTM **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in fast\-paced environments (e.g. high\-growth fintech/tech company, management consulting) * Experience working with PM, Engineers, GTM, Accounting Team or Finance Team * Experience working with financial datasets * Experience with visualization tools * Experience with AI tools (Claude Code, Kai, etc.) * Domain knowledge in FinTech Products * Qualification in Accounting/Finance or CPA/CFA certification In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €69,600 \- €104,400\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Data Architect – Azure
description: My client is seeking a Data Architect with strong expertise in data modelling and a proven track record designing ETL architectures. This is an opportunity to future\-proof your career with a global IT leader at the forefront of innovation and AI. **Key Responsibilities** * Translate business requirements into scalable data solutions, including data integration and transformation. * Define and promote data architecture principles within Azure. * Design modern, reference data architectures aligned to business needs. * Develop and implement ETL pipelines based on best\-practice architectures. * Lead migration of on\-premise solutions to Azure environments. * Set standards for data modelling and data warehouse design. * Establish processes and governance for data architecture practices. - **Essential Skills \& Experience** * Strong background in software development and Agile delivery. * Experience building data engineering solutions and pipelines. * Deep knowledge of data architecture patterns across descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics. * Expertise in data modelling: relational (Inmon) and dimensional (Kimball), including SCD (Types 1 \& 2\). * Hands\-on experience with modern data platforms: Data Lakes, Delta Lakes, Data Warehouses, Data Marts, and Lakehouses. * Proven experience with Azure data services (e.g., Azure Data Lake). * Ability to design and implement conceptual, logical, and physical data models. * Strong documentation, communication, and organisational skills. * Experience working across diverse IT environments and platforms. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. Relocation Package. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin. (2\-3 days onsite per week)** . Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Data Center Operation Engineer (Graduate Program)-Dublin, Ireland
description: Alibaba Cloud is hiring students who are enthusiastic and eager to learn and grow to join the data center operation and maintenance team. As a data center O \& M personnel, you will be responsible for maintaining, upgrading, and managing the infrastructure, servers, assets, and security of the data center to ensure the stable operation of the data center. Through close cooperation with experienced technical experts and managers, you will continuously improve your technical and management capabilities. As a data center O \& M personnel, you will have the following responsibilities: * Develop standardized operation management procedures covering Facilities, IT, Assets, and Security for Alibaba Cloud’s large\-scale IDC data centers. * Explore and apply advanced O\&M technologies in digitalization, automation, and intelligence. * Oversee execution of operation standards for incidents, issues, changes, risks, maintenance, and personnel to achieve performance targets. * Drive internal and external communication and collaboration to optimize resource utilization, align with business goals, and meet customer SLA requirements. * Ensure the safety, stability, and efficiency of data center operations. * Contribute to R\&D, planning, solution design, construction, and delivery of IDC infrastructure (energy, buildings, power, HVAC, monitoring, networks, servers, etc.). * Stay current with technological trends and support the evolution of data center architecture to strengthen Alibaba Cloud IDC’s competitiveness. **Job Requirements** * Solid knowledge of computer software, hardware, and IT fundamentals; understanding of foundational IT network architecture; ability in data analysis and mining. * Strong learning ability, curiosity about emerging technologies, and adaptability to new environments. * Excellent communication, teamwork, and independent problem\-solving skills. * Fluent in Chinese and English (listening, speaking, reading, and writing) as a working language, or proficiency in other local languages. * Internship or project experience in cloud computing or the data center industry is preferred.

title: Data Moderation Associate with French
description: **Data Moderation Associate** **Location** : Dublin 3\. Do you want to be part of a success story ? Cognizant has been a tremendous success story globally over the last 20\+ years. It is a member of the NASDAQ\-100, the S\&P 500, the Forbes Global 2000, and the Fortune 500 and is ranked among the top performing and fastest growing companies in the world. We run a multilingual Customer Support and Content Review Centre, for Technology Clients in Dublin. It is an instantly recognizable, global iconic brand and a place where employees learn, collaborate and help improve a digital product used by over a billion people worldwide! You will be part of a rapidly growing multilingual team and will work alongside other internal teams, for a leading online multinational company, to respond to requests and assist in the success of this project. We are committed to investing in our people. We offer a range of training programs at the start and throughout your career with Cognizant to increase your depth of knowledge on world\-class platforms and tools. You will have an opportunity to collaborate with a wide variety of stakeholders, both locally and globally. **Our Benefits Include** * Attractive salary * Performance based bonus scheme * Permanent contract * Rewards scheme * Private Medical Insurance * Competitive maternity benefits of 12 weeks paid Leave * Life assurance **Requirements** * Fluency in English and the required role language * General country specific knowledge for the country you will support * Be eligible to work in Ireland (Stamp 4, Stamp 1G or EU passport) * Computer fluency * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Customer engagement is the critical part of this role * Review data to assess accuracy against client policies * Engage with business, predominantly via outbound phone calls, to verify their information and update client platforms as appropriate. * Adhere to all compliance requirements * Ability to work effectively in a dynamic and fast paced environment Key success factors for this role are adaptability and flexibility, attention to detail and an ability to focus on repetitive tasks, excellent communication skills, an ability to make judgement and a problem\-solving nature. We offer interesting work at a globally admired technology company, an attractive compensation package, valuable training and career progression opportunities across the globe. Previous experience in a similar role is desired but not required.

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description: **Company Overview:** Holafly is a high\-growth scale\-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500\+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity. We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them. **The Role:** You will be the architect of our cloud foundations, ensuring security is baked into our DNA rather than bolted on as an afterthought. Your work directly enables our engineering teams to scale safely, protecting the connectivity of millions of global travellers. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design and operate the GCP cloud platform foundations, owning the full lifecycle of infrastructure and security. * Automate everything via Terraform and Ansible, eliminating "snowflake" servers and manual console configurations. * Maintain and scale GKE clusters, managing workloads, network policies, and cluster lifecycles. * Strengthen our security posture by implementing least\-privilege IAM, image scanning, and SOPS\-based secret management. * Optimise CI/CD pipelines using Cloud Build and GitHub to ensure high\-quality, secure code deployments. * Enhance observability and incident response through OpenTelemetry and OpenSearch to ensure 24/7 platform reliability. **Qualifications:** * Proven experience managing Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Kubernetes (GKE) at scale. * Expertise in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) specifically using Terraform. * Strong proficiency in Python or Bash for high\-level automation and tooling. * Deep understanding of container security, including Docker and artifact vulnerability scanning. * A "security\-first" mindset with experience in secret management and supply\-chain hygiene. **Nice to Have:** * Experience with OpenTelemetry or OpenSearch instrumentation. * Familiarity with SonarQube for automated code quality gates. * Previous experience in high\-growth scale\-ups or the travel\-tech industry. **Hybrid Modality** We focus on trust and performance. We are implementing a Two phased approach for our Hybrid Modality: Phase 1: While the teams are establishing ways of working, building trust, and developing their rhythm, we ask that you’re present in the office Three days a week. In\-person proximity is key to accelerating the forming stage. Phase 2: Once teams are performing, we move to a more relaxed hybrid model. We ask for a minimum of one day per week in the office, centred around critical ceremonies such as sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. **Benefits \& Perks** * 25 Days of PTO * 10% pension contribution * € 3000 for Medical/Health coverage * Self development budget of $ 500

title: Educational Advisor (B2C Sales)
description: **The Role** Code Institute is changing careers across EMEA \- helping people move into software development, data, and AI at a time when those skills have never been more in demand. We're proud to be rated Ofsted Outstanding and recognised as the Best Bootcamp in Europe, independently validated markers of the quality we hold ourselves to. As an Educational Edvisor, you're the person who upholds that standard at the very first conversation. You'll be the first meaningful touchpoint a prospective student has with us, and the quality of that conversation directly shapes whether they take a life\-changing step, and whether we hit our growth targets. We need someone who can hold a high\-volume, high\-activity rhythm and bring genuine consultative depth to every conversation. You'll be working with warm inbound leads, so the emphasis is on conversion quality \- understanding what a prospective student actually needs, being honest about fit, and closing with confidence when the match is right. **What You'll Be Doing** * Converting warm inbound leads at pace, maintaining the consistent daily activity levels needed to hit monthly and quarterly targets without cutting corners on quality. * Running consultative discovery conversations to understand a prospect's goals, background, and readiness \- matching them to the right programme accordingly. * Owning your pipeline end to end. Accurate CRM hygiene, forecasting, and deal progression are how we run the business. * Delivering tailored pitches that position Code Institute's programmes as high\-value career investments in AI, data, and software development. * Collaborating with Marketing and Product to share what you're hearing. Your insight shapes how we go to market. * Contributing to team culture \- knowledge sharing, peer support, and doing right by students are part of how we operate. **What We're Looking For** * 3\+ years B2C sales experience in a fast\-paced environment \- SaaS, EdTech, Insurance or similar. This is a firm requirement. * Genuinely consultative by nature \- you ask good questions, listen well, and will strive to match the right student to the right programme. * Able to sustain high activity levels without it showing in your quality. Rejection is part of the job and you've made peace with that. * Commercially driven \- you know your numbers, own your KPIs, and hitting targets genuinely matters to you. * Real interest in education, careers, or tech \- you don't need to be a developer, but the subject matter should engage you. * Clear, credible communicator across a wide range of prospects, from career changers to corporate L\&D buyers. **What We Offer** * Competitive base with uncapped OTE. * Remote\-first flexibility \- based in the UK or Ireland, we trust you to manage your day and deliver results. * A genuinely supportive team culture \- high\-energy and target\-driven, with real investment in coaching, development, and each other. * Meaningful work \- the outcomes you drive have a direct, measurable impact on people's lives and careers. Our Ofsted Outstanding rating and Best Bootcamp in Europe recognition reflect a genuine commitment to student outcomes that runs through everything we do. We take performance seriously and celebrate success, but we're equally serious about doing right by the students we enrol. If you're motivated purely by closing volume with no regard for outcome quality, this probably isn't your place. If you want to compete hard and feel genuinely good about the work, this could be for you! ***Code Institute is an equal opportunities employer and prides itself on diversity and inclusivity across our staff and our student body alike.***

title: Electrical & Instrumentation Apprenticeship
description: **Electrical \& Instrumentation Apprenticeship – Boliden Tara Mines** **Build a skilled, future\-ready career with support, purpose and real opportunities to grow.** At Boliden Tara Mines, we provide the metals essential to improve society for generations to come. We are guided by our values of Care, Courage and Responsibility, and by our ambition to be the most climate\-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. If you enjoy problem solving, technology, and learning how complex systems work \- this apprenticeship could be for you. **Thinking about your next step ?** An **Electrical \& Instrumentation Apprenticeship** at Tara Mines offers a practical, structured way to **earn while you learn** . This is a **4‑year paid apprenticeship programme** combining on‑the‑job learning with off‑site training. On successful completion, apprentices will achieve a **QQI Advanced Certificate – Level 6** , a nationally recognised qualification valued across multiple industries. **What You’ll Learn And Do (your Responsibilities)** With training and support, you’ll help keep critical process\-control systems safe, reliable and efficient, while building strong technical skills. * Install, test and calibrate instruments and control equipment * Work with sensors, transmitters and automation systems * Troubleshoot faults and resolve technical issues * Use digital tools and modern control technologies * Work safely to Boliden standards, support risk assessments and safety initiatives * Keep accurate maintenance records; contribute to housekeeping and 5S * Take part in daily team meetings, share ideas, and look for improvements in safety, sustainability and efficiency These skills are valued in mining, pharmaceuticals, food production and manufacturing. **Your support and development** Boliden Tara Mines offers world‑class technical training, along with competitive pay and excellent rewards as you progress and develop your skills. You will be part of a safety\-focused, inclusive team, supported by experienced and qualified craftspeople who will: * Coach and guide you throughout your apprenticeship * Help you build confidence in your technical skills * Encourage questions and continuous learning We take pride in creating an environment where apprentices feel supported, respected and safe to learn. **Entry Requirements** * Aged 18 years or over * Leaving Certificate with a minimum of five passes at Ordinary Level, including English and Maths **Skills And Qualities We Look For** * Curiosity and interest in how things work * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and ask questions * A positive, proactive approach * An interest in technology, engineering or problem solving **Practical details** * 39 hours per week, Monday to Friday * Some flexibility required in line with operational needs * A structured apprenticeship combining on\-the\-job learning with off\-site training **Opportunities within the wider Boliden Group** An apprenticeship with Boliden Tara Mines offers more than a qualification — it’s the start of a long‑term career. Graduates can grow onsite and across the **wider Boliden Group** , with skills valued nationally and internationally. **Inclusion matters to us** At Boliden Tara Mines, we are committed to a workplace where women and men feel welcome, supported and confident to succeed. We encourage applications from all backgrounds, including people who may not have previously considered a career in Electrical Instrumentation If you’re interested but unsure whether you meet all the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. **If this opportunity interests you, we welcome your application via Boliden Careers.** **Closing date: 19 June 2026** Important! If you are a current employee within Boliden you shouldn't complete the application at this site, please log on to your Workday account and apply through "Find Jobs".

title: Facilities Officer
description: **About the Role** The Facilities Department is responsible for the provision of a range of services, including: * The safe and comfortable occupation (by NTA staff and other accommodated personnel) of the NTA’s office location in Haymarket House; * Planning of office accommodation supply to meet the current and future needs of the NTA; and * Work activities carried out in or by persons based in the NTA’s office location. **Duties and Responsibilities** * Assist in the work and administration of the facilities division. This includes ordering office supplies, building management, post management, and supporting the Facilities Manager in the management and delivery of projects; * Proactively manage the facilities Helpdesk ensuring all requests are logged and completed in a timely manner; * Maintain the Facilities Contract Register to the highest standard, to contain up\-to\-date information relating to all contracts with third party suppliers of facilities services; * Raise requisitions and assist in the management of budget for the properties, including rental and maintenance costs; * Carry out daily/weekly building inspections ensuring all office accommodation is maintained to a high standard and taking action where required; * Provide administration support for a variety of vendor documentation, including maintenance, catering, security, cleaning and plant equipment vendors, waste management and car parking; * Provide assistance in recording sustainability data; * Apply a continuous improvement approach and support ad hoc projects as they arise; * Lead by example in respect of occupational health \& safety and compliance with statutory and contractual legislative requirements; * Assist with space planning, coordinating desk moves and maintaining up to date seating plans; * Support the development and implementation of occupational health and safety programs; * Assist with maintaining and updating the register of all First Aiders and Fire Wardens, and organise refresher training courses as necessary; * Liaise with Human Resources regarding employee requirements; * Sports and Social club \- arranging activities for NTA staff; and * Any other tasks assigned to the role. **Note :** The functions and responsibilities initially assigned to the position are based on the current organisational requirements and may be changed from time to time. The person appointed requires the flexibility to fulfil other roles and responsibilities at a similar level within the NTA. **Essential Criteria** **Please note: In order to satisfy the shortlisting panel that you meet these criteria you must explicitly reference how you meet same in your application. Failure to demonstrate these may prevent your application progressing to future shortlisting stages.** Each candidate must meet the following requirements at the time of the competition closing: 1. Have a minimum of a NFQ level 4 qualification or equivalent; 2. Have a minimum 2 years’ experience in facilities administration in a corporate environment; 3. Have demonstrable experience in the area of occupational health and safety in a corporate environment; 4. Have proficient IT skills, including Microsoft Office applications, to include MS Word and MS Excel; and 5. Have strong administrative and organisation skills. **Desirable Criteria** **Please note: Should further shortlisting be required after essential criteria above, a selection of the following may be assessed.** The ideal candidate will also: 1. Have a proven ability to develop and maintain good working relationships with fellow colleagues and others, within and outside the organisation; 2. Have strong communication skills and the ability to negotiate both internally and externally with contractors, suppliers, and maintenance providers; and 3. Demonstrate the ability to prioritise and manage conflicting needs of the business. **Remuneration** **Salary Grade: Executive Officer** **Salary Scale: €38,419, €40,360, €41,456, €43,594, €45,510, €47,364, €49,211, €51,024, €52,890, €54,749, €56,722, €58,044, €59,928 (LSI 1\), €62,601 (LSI 2\)** **Personal Pension Contribution (PPC) rate.** This salary is payable to an individual who is required to make a personal pension contribution (PPC) to their main pension (in general those persons whose initial appointment to the Public Service is on or after 6th April 1995\). **€36,843, €39,034, €39,900, €41,769, €43,586, €45,350, €47,102, €48,819, €50,556, €52,263, €54,066, €55,299, €57,069 (LSI 1\), €59,592 (LSI 2\)** **Non Personal Pension Contribution (non\-PPC) rate.** This salary is payable to an individual who is not required to make a personal pension contribution (PPC) to their main pension scheme. **Annual Leave:** 23 days per annum. This leave is on the basis of a five day week and is exclusive of the usual public holidays. **Note:** * entry will be at point 1 of the scale and will not be subject to negotiation; * different pay and conditions may apply if, immediately prior to appointment the appointee is already a serving Civil Servant or Public Servant; * the rate of remuneration may be adjusted from time to time in line with Government pay policy. **Contract:** Permanent Contract **Probation:** There is a 6 month probationary period which may at the discretion of the CEO be extended to 10 months. **Selection Process** Prior to completing your application please read the Important Candidate Information Booklet on our careers page here: **www.nationaltransport.ie/about\-us/careers** **How to Apply** Please submit your application in one single word document or PDF referencing the title of the role you wish to apply for in the subject of the email to **NTA\-Careers@nationaltransport.ie** with the following: 1. A comprehensive cover letter outlining why you wish to be considered for the post and where you believe your skills and experience meet the requirements for the role of Facilities Officer; and 2. A comprehensive CV (not to exceed 3 pages). Please note that omission of any or part of the 2 requested documents, as set out above, will render the application incomplete. Incomplete applications will not be considered for the next stage of the selection process. **Closing Date** **The closing date and time for applications is strictly 12pm (noon) on Friday, 22 May 2026\.Applications received after the specified deadline cannot be accepted.** If you do not receive an acknowledgement of receipt of your application within 2 working days of applying, please email **NTA\-Careers@nationaltransport.ie.**

title: Finance Administrator

title: Financial Accountant
description: **The Company** Cubic³ provides advanced software\-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in\-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. **The Role** As our new **Financial Accountant**, you will be at the heart of Cubic’s Finance Operations function – delivering accurate financial reporting, strengthening controls, and providing the insights that enable confident decision\-making across the Group. As a company, Cubic3 provides a managed IoT connectivity platform, global carrier network access, and tailored connected services to leading automotive and mobile asset OEMs worldwide. This role sits within a finance team that is responsible for the timely, accurate and compliant reporting of these operations. The team is accountable for the consolidated Group results of 20\+ subsidiaries across the world, external and internal audit, local subsidiary management of accounting and tax providers, and periodic reporting to our investors, including one of Japan’s largest PLCs. We’re looking for a qualified accountant who combines strong technical foundations with genuine curiosity and a passion for challenging the status quo. As a valued member of the Finance team, you’ll work cross\-functionally with Legal, Commercial, FP\&A and the Product \& Tech teams – building the kind of broad commercial awareness that accelerates long\-term career growth. This is a hands\-on role with real scope to influence how the finance function evolves as Cubic scales. This role promises the skills and opportunity for future career development to an ambitious accountant. The candidate’s key duties will include: Financial reporting and month\-end close * Lead an assigned set of month\-end close activities to ensure accurate and timely financial reporting, including preparation of journal entries, accruals, and variance analysis. * Take ownership of group\-level reporting by uploading and consolidating financials for our parent company, SoftBank – providing direct exposure to global corporate governance and reporting frameworks. * Prepare management reports that go beyond the numbers – delivering deep analysis of key metrics, trends, and variances that influence business direction. Balance sheet and reconciliations * Manage and review complex balance sheet reconciliations, maintaining financial integrity and strengthening technical accounting skills through real\-world application. * Perform regular reconciliation and analysis of financial data, identifying discrepancies and driving timely resolution. Compliance and tax * Oversee statutory reporting and end\-to\-end tax compliance (VAT, VIES, MOSS, RTD), gaining hands\-on experience with both domestic and cross\-border tax obligations. * Support the preparation of statutory financial statements and ensure adherence to relevant accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Audit * Act as a primary Finance contact for external and internal audits (including with SoftBank), building insight into risk management and compliance at an international level. * Prepare audit schedules, respond to auditor queries, and drive the timely completion of audit deliverables. Cash flow and forecasting * Play a central role in safeguarding liquidity by monitoring daily cash activity and delivering forecasts that support informed operational and investment planning. * Work closely with the Financial Controller on cash forecasting analysis and treasury activities. Process improvement and automation * Identify automation opportunities across the finance function, reducing manual effort and increasing strategic value – including exploring how AI can our enhance day\-to\-day processes. * Champion best practices and embed continuous improvement into the team’s ways of working. Business partnering * Partner with the FP\&A team on budgeting, forecasting, and financial modelling – a powerful development opportunity for any accountant looking to broaden into commercial finance. * Collaborate cross\-functionally with Legal, Commercial, Software Engineering, and Product to ensure the finance function supports the wider business effectively. **Requirements** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent) with a solid technical foundation and commercial awareness * Minimum of two years’ post\-qualification experience in a dynamic finance environment, ideally within a fast\-paced or international business * Proven hands\-on experience with month\-end close processes, financial reporting, or audit preparation * Strong financial acumen with the ability to scrutinise, validate, and sense\-check complex financial data with confidence * Versatile team player – enthusiastic, flexible, pragmatic, thorough, and proactive **Skills \& Attributes that will set you apart:** * **Detail\-driven mindset** with high standards for accuracy, quality, and integrity * **Systems\-savvy** – experience with Oracle NetSuite is a strong advantage, but a quick learner on ERP platforms is welcome * **Adaptable and collaborative** – thrives in a team environment while also confident working independently * **Naturally curious and proactive** – keeps up to date with evolving accounting standards and technology innovations * **Strong communicator** – comfortable translating numbers into insights for finance and non\-finance stakeholders alike * **Self\-starter with initiative** – enjoys taking ownership and driving continuous improvement This is an opportunity to join a best\-in\-class Finance Operations function in a high\-growth, global connectivity business with direct exposure to international reporting, cross\-functional collaboration, and the commercial drivers behind Cubic’s ambitious growth trajectory. *New hires at* Cubic³ *are required to work onsite five days a week during their six\-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working.
